description_long: This is a rectangular, single-story wooden cabin with a light beige exterior and a gray metal gable roof. The structure features a prominent side extension with a smaller gable roof, and the main building is elevated on a stone foundation. Multiple evenly spaced rectangular windows line the longer facades, while the shorter sides have fewer or no windows. There are several wooden steps leading up to the doors, and the cabin sits on a grassy plot with a defined base. The overall appearance suggests a traditional, utilitarian design suitable for residential or communal use.
description: A beige wooden cabin with a gray gable roof, stone foundation, and multiple rectangular windows, set on a grassy base.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main entrance with steps, the side extension, and several windows along the facade.
description_view_1: The left side view highlights the narrow extension and the length of the main roof, with minimal window visibility.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a long row of windows and a single door, emphasizing the building's rectangular shape.
description_view_3: The right side view shows the solid end wall of the cabin with no visible windows or doors.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with a secondary gable extension, constructed from metal panels
attribute_window_arrangement: Multiple medium-sized rectangular windows are symmetrically distributed along the longer facades, with three on the front and five on the rear, and none visible on the right side; the extension has one window on its front-facing side.
attribute_door_placement: Two exterior doors are visible: one on the front facade near the extension and one on the rear facade at the far right end.
